name: Belmont Bank & Trust Company
description_short: FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services with locations in Chicago and Florida.
description_long: Belmont Bank & Trust Company is a federally-insured bank that has established itself as a full-service financial institution serving both personal and business banking needs. The bank is FDIC-insured, meaning customer deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government up to applicable limits. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including multiple checking account options (Kasasa Cash Checking, Kasasa Cash Back Checking, Belmont Checking, NOW Accounts), personal savings accounts, money market accounts, Health Savings Accounts (HSAs), Certificates of Deposit (CDs), IRAs, and credit products including personal loans and Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs). For business customers, Belmont provides corporate checking, business savings, business money market accounts, Interest on Lawyer Trust Accounts (IOLTA), business loans, lines of credit, equipment financing, and commercial real estate loans. Both segments have access to digital banking tools including Apple Pay, surcharge-free ATM networks, Zelle transfers, and treasury management services. What distinguishes Belmont is their emphasis on technology-enabled convenience and reward-based checking accounts through the Kasasa platform, which allows customers to earn monthly cash rewards simply by meeting basic account activity requirements. The bank also provides identity protection through Kasasa Protect, free credit score monitoring, and positions itself as a relationship-driven lender with the tagline "Your Partner In Business™." Their treasury management services, remote deposit, ACH services, and lockbox options appeal to business owners seeking operational efficiency. However, as a traditional brick-and-mortar bank, Belmont likely cannot compete with online-only banks on deposit rates or loan terms. The website content is primarily marketing-focused and lacks specific information about interest rates, fees, loan terms, credit requirements, or minimum balance thresholds (beyond noting small business checking requires no minimum). Customers should directly contact the bank or visit branches to compare rates and terms with competitors before opening accounts.
services: Personal checking (Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, Belmont Checking, NOW Accounts)
Personal savings (Statement Savings, Money Market, HSA, CDs, IRAs, CDARS & ICS)
Business checking (Corporate Checking, Small Business Checking, IOLTA)
Business savings (Statement Savings, Business Money Market)
Personal loans and Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOC)
Business loans (commercial, equipment term, lines of credit, commercial real estate)
Digital banking (Apple Pay, Zelle, online banking, mobile banking)
Treasury management (ACH services, remote deposit, lockbox services, positive pay)
Credit products (personal and business credit cards)
Identity protection (Kasasa Protect with credit monitoring)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits protected by U.S. government backing
Free Kasasa Cash and Kasasa Cash Back checking accounts that pay monthly interest rewards
Surcharge-free ATM network access across multiple locations
Free credit score monitoring available through mobile app
Comprehensive digital banking including Apple Pay, Zelle, and mobile/online banking
Business-focused treasury management tools including ACH, remote deposit, and lockbox services
Multiple loan products for both personal (HELOC, personal loans) and business (SBA, equipment, C&I, commercial real estate)
cons: No published interest rates, fees, or APRs on website to compare with competitors
Limited geographic footprint with locations only in Chicago, IL and Coral Gables, FL
Website lacks transparency on minimum balance requirements, overdraft policies, and account terms
As traditional bank, likely cannot match rates offered by online-only banking competitors
No information provided about business loan qualification requirements or approval timeline
best_for: Small business owners seeking local relationship banking with treasury management tools
Personal customers wanting free checking with monthly cash rewards tied to account activity
Businesses requiring specialized services like IOLTA accounts or commercial real estate financing
Customers in Chicago or South Florida seeking FDIC-insured banking with digital convenience
diagnosis: Belmont Bank & Trust is best for small business owners and personal customers in Chicago or South Florida seeking relationship-based banking with competitive digital tools and free rewards checking. The main caveat is lack of published rates and terms on their website—customers must contact the bank directly to compare their offerings against online competitors, and geographic limitation to two regions may limit accessibility for customers outside these areas.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
